explanation: The analyzer worries that 'argv[3]' is null when passed to atoi at line 31, but the code returns early unless argc == 4. When argc == 4 the C runtime guarantees argv[0]..argv[3] are non-null, so argv[3] cannot be null here. False positive.

Reaching getline at line 33 with reader->file == NULL requires already having misused the NULL FILE* at the earlier feof(reader->file) call at line 53. This alert is therefore dependent on that earlier defect; fixing the missing fopen() check removes both.
Variable 'tmp' at line 42 is assigned but never read on any path before it goes out of scope. This is a genuine, if minor, dead-store / unused-variable issue.
